params.description: Hundred Grid Mode B appears displaying a 4 × 5 rectangle (4 columns, 5 rows, 20 square tiles total). Build affordances are disabled — rectangle is static reference visual only.
Multiple Choice / Checkbox interface appears showing four equation candidates.

beats.text: Look at this rectangle. You know it shows 4 times 5 equals 20. But there's more than one way to write that fact.
Choose ALL the equations that correctly describe this rectangle.

validator.description: Student selected both correct equations: 4 × 5 = 20 and 20 = 4 × 5

beats.text: Both of those describe the same rectangle — two ways to write the same fact.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode B showing static 4 × 5 rectangle (20 tiles).
Multiple Choice / Checkbox interface with four equation options: 4 × 5 = 20, 20 = 4 × 5, 4 + 5 = 20, 4 × 20 = 5. Student has selected both correct equations.

params.description: Drop Down / Fill-in-the-Blank prompt appears with completion stem.

beats.text: Now finish this sentence about why both equations work.

options: they show two ways to write the same fact
the equal sign means both sides have the same value
one of them must be wrong

condition.selected: they show two ways to write the same fact

beats.text: Two ways to write the same fact. The equal sign lets you write multiplication either way.

condition.selected: the equal sign means both sides have the same value

beats.text: Both sides have the same value. That's what the equal sign tells you.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode B showing static 4 × 5 rectangle (20 tiles).
Multiple Choice / Checkbox interface showing selected equations: 4 × 5 = 20 and 20 = 4 × 5.
Drop Down / Fill-in-the-Blank prompt completed with one of the two reinforcing options selected.
